DISCOVERY, 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF RECORDS DIRECTED TO: 1) UCSF MEDICAL CENTER; 2) STEUART GOLD, MTF(SIC) AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER RESTRAINING AND EXCLUDING IRRLEVANT AND PRIVILEGED EVIDENCE. OFF CALENDAR, AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. CLAIM FOR PROMISSORY FRAUD SUFFICIENT. ALTHOUGH ONE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES TO SECTION 970 CLAIM, IT BEGINS TO ACCRUE WHEN THE EMPLOYEE LOSES HER JOB, NOT EARLIER WHEN SHE DISCOVERS THE FRAUD. AGUILERA VS. PIRELLI (2000) 223 F.3D 1010, 1018. THE COURT ADDS: THIS RULING WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MOVING PARTY BRINGING A FURTHER MOTION RE: ISSUE OF STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA LERTORA, CSR #11935

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION ARE WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE INVESTIGATION WHICH CAN REASONABLY BE EXPECTED TO GROW OUT OF THE CHARGE OF DISCRIMINATION. THE INITIAL ADMINISTRATIVE COMPLAINT REFERENCES "DENIED ACCOMMODATION" AND THAT BGI WOULD NOT ACCOMMODATE PLAINTIFF'S RETURN FROM MEDICAL LEAVE BY REDUCING HER HOURS." MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT RETALIATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: John A. McGuinn, Esq. of McGuinn, Hillsman & Palefsky, (415) 421-9292 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Daniel J. McCoy, Esq. of Fenwick & West LLP (650) 335-7897 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication denied as to disability discrimination cause of action. The allegations of this cause of action are within the scope of the administrative investigation which can reasonably be expected to grow out of the charge of discrimination. The initial administrative complaint references "denied accommodation" and that BGI would not accommodate plaintiff's return from medical leave by reducing her hours." Motion for summary adjudication denied as to retaliation cause of action, failure to shift burden of proof. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support retaliation. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint (First Cause Of Action -Promissory Fraud And Fifth Cause Of Action--Violation Of Cal. Labor Code 970); Compendium Of Non-California Authorities In Support Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA Demurrer to First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Dan Obuhanych, Esq. of Fenwick & West LLP, (650) 988-8500 appearing for Defendant and Moving party; John A. McGuinn, Esq. of McQuinn, Hillsman & Palefsky (415) 421-9292, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Claim for promissory fraud sufficient. Although one-year statute of limitations applies to section 970 claim, it begins to accrue when the employee loses her job, not earlier when she discovers the fraud. Aguilera vs. Pirelli (2000) 223 F.3d 1010, 1018. The Court adds: This ruling without prejudice to moving party bringing a further motion re: issue of statute of limitations. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935
